subject: ap-psychology
title: Social Psychology — Social Facilitation and Performance (EBQ)

parts.prompt: Propose a specific and defensible claim based in psychological science that responds to the question.
parts.scoring_notes: • A claim that meets the criteria can be awarded the point regardless of whether the responses in Parts B and C successfully support the claim.

scoring_points.description: Proposes a claim that is relevant to the question

required_elements: “Does the presence of others influence performance?”
alternatives.correct_example: “Does the presence of others influence performance?”

required_elements: “Using animals in research can tell us a lot about ourselves.”
alternatives.correct_example: “Using animals in research can tell us a lot about ourselves.”

required_elements: “The presence of others makes a difference in whether someone performs well on a specific task or set of tasks.”
alternatives.correct_example: “The presence of others makes a difference in whether someone performs well on a specific task or set of tasks.”

required_elements: “When someone else is present during a performance, it is
alternatives.correct_example: “When someone else is present during a performance, it is

required_elements: “Studying the factors that influence performance is more likely that the participant will perform well.” important to social psychologists.”
alternatives.correct_example: “Studying the factors that influence performance is more likely that the participant will perform well.” important to social psychologists.”

required_elements: “The presence of others will make them do worse at what they are doing.”
alternatives.correct_example: “The presence of others will make them do worse at what they are doing.”

required_elements: “People should have an audience for tasks they are good at.”
alternatives.correct_example: “People should have an audience for tasks they are good at.”

parts.prompt: Support your claim using at least one piece of specific and relevant evidence from one of the sources. ii. Explain how the evidence from Part B (i) supports your claim using a psychological perspective, theory, concept, or research finding learned in AP Psychology.
parts.scoring_notes: • Responses that use a correct citation style other than the methods prescribed by the question can earn this point for citing the source. • “Accurate evidence” refers to the accuracy of the general pattern of the evidence cited. Since citing specific numbers is not required to score the point, slight errors in

scoring_points.description: Uses one piece of correctly cited, specific, accurate, and relevant evidence from one of the provided sources to support the claim.

required_elements: “The presence of others impacts what we do.” [No evidence.]
alternatives.correct_example: “The presence of others impacts what we do.” [No evidence.]

required_elements: “The baboon study shows that the presence of other baboons made the baboons do worse on the task that required more cognitive control.” [No citation.]
alternatives.correct_example: “The baboon study shows that the presence of other baboons made the baboons do worse on the task that required more cognitive control.” [No citation.]

required_elements: “The baboons were influenced by other baboons.”
alternatives.correct_example: “The baboons were influenced by other baboons.”

required_elements: “In Source 2, researchers used rewards to change the baboon’s behavior.” [Evidence not relevant to the claim.]
alternatives.correct_example: “In Source 2, researchers used rewards to change the baboon’s behavior.” [Evidence not relevant to the claim.]

required_elements: “The results of the bar graph (Source 1) show a longer amount of time for participants to complete a new and difficult task in front of an audience than when they were alone.”
alternatives.correct_example: “The results of the bar graph (Source 1) show a longer amount of time for participants to complete a new and difficult task in front of an audience than when they were alone.”

required_elements: “According to Source 3, performance was improved when participants were observed by an evaluator and when they were monitored electronically.”
alternatives.correct_example: “According to Source 3, performance was improved when participants were observed by an evaluator and when they were monitored electronically.”

required_elements: “According to Source 2, researchers found that the delayed reaction time was greatest for male baboons in the presence of older males with a higher social rank.”
alternatives.correct_example: “According to Source 2, researchers found that the delayed reaction time was greatest for male baboons in the presence of older males with a higher social rank.”

required_elements: “According to Source 1, participants were able to perform a well-learned task quicker than those who were alone.”
alternatives.correct_example: “According to Source 1, participants were able to perform a well-learned task quicker than those who were alone.”

parts.prompt: Explain how the evidence from Part B(i) supports your claim using a psychological perspective, theory, concept, or research finding learned in AP Psychology.
parts.scoring_notes: • The explanation and application points can be earned even if the response did not earn the point in B (i) due to incorrectly cited and/or non-specific evidence. • The psychological perspective, theory, concept, or research finding must be explicitly identified in the AP Psychology Course and Exam Description (2024). • The presence of a citation indicates the sentence or phrase is intended to be

scoring_points.description: Partial credit (1 pt): Uses evidence from the study but does not fully explain how the concept is supported or refuted, or explains without using a specific research finding.

required_elements: Explains the relationship between the evidence and the claim

scoring_points.description: Full credit (2 pts): Uses a specific result from the study to accurately explain how the results support or refute the psychological concept, with correctly interpreted evidence.

required_elements: Explains the relationship between the evidence and the claim

common_traps: Must accurately interpret results — not just cite evidence
Must explicitly identify the psychological concept/theory from the AP Psychology CED

parts.prompt: Support your claim using an additional piece of specific and relevant evidence from a different source than the one that was used in Part B (i). ii. Explain how the evidence from Part C (i) supports your claim using a different psychological perspective, theory, concept, or research finding learned in AP Psychology than the one that was used in Part B (ii).
parts.scoring_notes: • Responses that use a correct citation style other than the methods prescribed by the question can earn this point for citing the source. • “Accurate evidence” refers to the accuracy of the general pattern of the evidence cited. Since citing specific numbers is not required to score the point, slight errors in

scoring_points.description: Uses a different piece of correctly cited, specific, accurate, and relevant evidence from either of the other two provided sources to support the claim

required_elements: “The presence of others impacts what we do.” [No evidence.]
alternatives.correct_example: “The presence of others impacts what we do.” [No evidence.]

required_elements: “In one study, people who just thought they were being monitored performed better.” [No citation.]
alternatives.correct_example: “In one study, people who just thought they were being monitored performed better.” [No citation.]

required_elements: “Research shows that behavior changes when in a group.”
alternatives.correct_example: “Research shows that behavior changes when in a group.”

required_elements: “In Source 2, researchers found that baboons performed worse on the conflicting trial task when they were in the presence of other baboons.” [The response used the same source as used in Part B.]
alternatives.correct_example: “In Source 2, researchers found that baboons performed worse on the conflicting trial task when they were in the presence of other baboons.” [The response used the same source as used in Part B.]

required_elements: “In Source 2, researchers used rewards to change the baboon’s behavior.” [Evidence not relevant to the claim.]
alternatives.correct_example: “In Source 2, researchers used rewards to change the baboon’s behavior.” [Evidence not relevant to the claim.]

parts.prompt: Explain how the evidence from Part C(i) supports your claim using a different psychological perspective, theory, concept, or research finding than the one used in Part B(ii).
parts.scoring_notes: • The explanation and application points can be earned even if the response did not earn the point in C (i) due to incorrectly cited and/or non-specific evidence. • The explanation and application points can be earned if the response uses the same source in both Parts B and C, provided that the evidence used in Part B and Part C is different. • The psychological perspective, theory, concept, or re

required_elements: Explains the relationship between the evidence and the claim

scoring_points.description: Full credit (2 pts): Uses a specific result from the study to accurately explain how the results support or refute the psychological concept, with correctly interpreted evidence.
